title: Mildly Spicy Korean Pork Anju
description: A jammy and creamy Korean dish featuring cilantro leaves, perfect for adventurous eaters, this anju is wrapped in pandan leaves for a unique flavor and presentation.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together gochujang, soy sauce, brown sugar, and sesame oil to make the marinade.
Add the sliced pork to the marinade and mix well to coat, then cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat and cook the pork for 5-7 minutes per side, or until caramelized and cooked through.
While the pork is cooking, mix the chopped cilantro and minced garlic in a small bowl.
Once the pork is done, let it rest for a few minutes before slicing it thinly against the grain.
To assemble the anju, lay a pandan leaf flat and place a few slices of the pork in the center, then spoon some of the cilantro and garlic mixture on top.
Fold the pandan leaf over the filling to form a triangle or square shape, and press the edges together to seal.
Serve the anju warm, with additional cilantro and garlic mixture on the side if desired.
Repeat with the remaining ingredients to make 12 anju in total.
